source.description: A reviewed six-panel raster contact sheet showing one face-free harmonica from pale box construction to a bold marker finish.
source.notes: The same three-quarter view, rounded body, eight mouth holes, two end caps, two screws, three sound arcs, and shadow must persist after introduction.

process_strategy: Build one low rounded box, reserve both end caps, divide the mouth edge into exactly eight holes before inking, then add hardware, sound, saturated marker fill, and final line weight without changing the perspective.

final_elements.name: low rounded harmonica body and center axis
final_elements.description: A long low box with rounded corners tilted gently from lower left toward upper right.

final_elements.name: top and bottom cover plates with two end caps
final_elements.description: Two layered cover-plate bands and one compact cap at each end of the body.

final_elements.name: exactly eight rectangular mouth holes
final_elements.description: Eight evenly spaced dark openings along the near mouth edge.

final_elements.name: two round screws and two cover seams
final_elements.description: One screw near each end plus two short seams across the established top cover plate.

final_elements.name: three sound arcs and oval shadow
final_elements.description: Exactly three curved sound strokes above one end and one shallow oval shadow below the instrument.

final_elements.name: raspberry, cyan, yellow, and cool-gray marker fills
final_elements.description: Directional saturated fills with narrow white paper highlight gaps inside established shapes.

frames.visible_job: Uses a center axis and pale rounded box to set the harmonica's long three-quarter body and reserves the two end-cap footprints.
introduces: low rounded harmonica body and center axis

frames.step_name: Layer the covers and end caps
frames.visible_job: Adds the top and bottom cover plates plus one compact end cap at each end of the established body.
introduces: top and bottom cover plates with two end caps
requires_prior_elements: low rounded harmonica body and center axis

frames.visible_job: Places exactly eight rectangular openings in one even row along the near edge between the two established end caps.
requires_prior_elements: low rounded harmonica body and center axis
top and bottom cover plates with two end caps

frames.step_name: Add hardware and a bright note
frames.visible_job: Adds two round screws, two short cover seams, exactly three sound arcs, and one shallow oval shadow to the established instrument.
introduces: two round screws and two cover seams
three sound arcs and oval shadow
requires_prior_elements: low rounded harmonica body and center axis
top and bottom cover plates with two end caps

frames.visible_job: Thickens the existing black contours and fills the established cover, mouth, cap, hole, screw, sound, and shadow shapes with bright streaked marker color.
introduces: raspberry, cyan, yellow, and cool-gray marker fills
requires_prior_elements: low rounded harmonica body and center axis
top and bottom cover plates with two end caps
two round screws and two cover seams
three sound arcs and oval shadow

final_step.visible_job: Reinforces existing black lines, evens the established marker fills, and sharpens the eight holes, two caps, screws, seams, sound arcs, highlights, and shadow without adding new structure.
allowed_changes: second-pass marker fill inside existing color areas
small highlight cleanup inside established forms
slight deepening of the existing oval shadow

rejection_checks: Exactly eight mouth holes must remain visible from step 3 through the final.
Both end caps, two screws, two seams, three sound arcs, and the shadow may not drift or disappear.
No face, letters, musical notes, hands, microphone, sticker border, badge frame, or extra instrument may appear.
Color must appear before the finished panel and stay inside existing shapes.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final must show handmade marker streaks rather than vector-clean fills.
